vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry-defined specimens are largely absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (Yellow Pot): Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible; soil appears inert.
inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: The Mexican Mint and Silver Guest are no longer identifiable as living specimens; only debris remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: The Pothos and the white rabbit anchor are missing from the current frame.
inventory_reconciliation.New_Introductions: None. The environment is currently devoid of healthy botanical life.

plant_audit.P2_P4: Contains only white, calcified debris (likely eggshell fragments) and a single, withered, non-viable stem remnant in the earliest images, which has since vanished.
plant_audit.P3: The white rabbit anchor, present in the earliest images, has been removed or displaced.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears desiccated and compacted across all visible pots.
biome_observations.debris: Persistent presence of white, jagged fragments (eggshells) in the black pot, which remain static while the organic matter decays/disappears.
biome_observations.lighting_impact: The low-light, cool-spectrum environment has failed to support the specimens, leading to total senescence.

temporal_deltas.Earliest_to_T-4: Initial state shows a single green stem in the black pot and the white rabbit anchor in the P3 position.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-2: Gradual loss of structural integrity of the green stem; the white rabbit remains.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: Total collapse of the biome. The white rabbit is removed, and the plant matter has completely withered or been cleared, leaving only the soil and shell debris.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is effectively dead. There is no evidence of photosynthesis, turgor pressure, or active growth. The environment is currently a 'dead zone' of inert soil and inorganic debris.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshell fragments suggests an attempt at soil amendment or a non-botanical intervention that failed to prevent the total loss of the specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Over the course of the five-day observation, the specimens have undergone total senescence and removal. The current state is characterized by empty, dark pots and the absence of the scale anchor (white rabbit). The biome is no longer functional.
